plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure the concentration flag and reduce overall exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Reduce SMH exposure due to its downtrend status.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Hold a significant cash buffer to preserve capital.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to address concentration and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL and reduce SPAL to minimize leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Reduce CRWD slightly to manage single name concentration.
Add AMD as a new position based on its strong scanner setup.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing a new high-quality setup, but market swings are still expected.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure the concentration flag and manage gross leverage.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure, strictly with stops.
Sell SPAL to exit a position below its stop.
Reduce SMH exposure due to its downtrend status.
Add to CRWD, pressing into a working position with strong P&L.
Buy AMD, a top-scoring scanner setup, to increase growth exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth. It leans into leveraged ETFs and concentration, which amplifies both potential upside and drawdown. Daily decay and rebalancing in leveraged ETFs can lead to significant losses if stops are not strictly followed.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Sell SMH to exit a high-beta sector with a downtrend.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize monthly distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliable income.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
